_comment: Tab 1 (REGRESSION) chart specs for the Benchmark v2 Kafka dashboard, DWH Superset.
Consumed by the Stage-B build (script or browser operator). Layout follows
docs/benchmark-v2-plan.md §8 Tab 1 sketch: 5 tiles row, latest-pair gated table,
2 ratio-trend lines with band shading + provisional watermark, excursion+flagged log.
EVERY chart carries `description` (what it shows / how to read it / direction of
goodness) per the #33 normative directive. ADDITIVE-ONLY: all new assets; the
verdict tiles read verdict/provisional/alert_now from v_kc_pair_ratios so
CONTRACT LEVEL: Amendment 2026-07-09b (Stage A2, ruling option B): calibrated
per-metric bands (drain/null ±8.5%, connect-cpu ±6%, same both tiers),
parts_per_insert = binary TRIPWIRE (not a ratio band; excluded from the ratio
trends), merge_amplification watch-only (NOT on this tab's verdict artifacts;
reported on Tab 2), ch_avg_rows_per_insert degated (covariate in v_kc_runs).
drain_rate_stability is intentionally NOT charted (principal ruling, #33 constraint 3).

datasets.v_kc_pair_ratios: verdict view (per pair,tier,gated metric): verdict (OK/IMPROVEMENT/REGRESSION/NO_DATA/FLAGGED/TRIPWIRE), ratio, band, is_tripwire, provisional, alert_now, pair_ts, pair_seq
datasets.v_kc_runs: base fact (per run): integrity_ok, duplicate_rows, task_retries/restarts/rebalances, flagged, merge_amplification + ch_avg_rows_per_insert as covariates
datasets.v_kc_flagged_log: flagged + failed run log (audit trail for excluded runs)

charts.query: SELECT multiIf(countIf(verdict IN ('REGRESSION','TRIPWIRE'))>0, 'REGRESSION', countIf(verdict='NO_DATA')=count(), 'NO_DATA', 'OK') AS tier0_verdict FROM v_kc_pair_ratios WHERE tier='0' AND pair_seq=1
charts.subheader_source: worst-of the Tier-0 gated metrics for the latest pair; 'provisional — calibrating (n=X/20)' watermark when any row provisional=1
charts.description: TIER 0 verdict tile for the latest pair. Rolls the Tier-0 gated metrics (null_drain_rows_per_sec, banded ±8.5%; connect_cpu_seconds_per_Mrows, banded ±6%) into one worst-case verdict: bad if any metric regressed, else NO_DATA if all are no-data, else OK. HOW TO READ: green OK = last night's Tier-0 pair held its calibrated bands; red = a Tier-0 metric moved outside its band in the BAD direction. While calibrating it shows 'provisional — calibrating (n=X/20)' and band excursions do NOT alert. DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: OK/IMPROVEMENT are good; REGRESSION is bad; NO_DATA/FLAGGED are neither (investigate, not a regression).

charts.query: SELECT multiIf(countIf(verdict='TRIPWIRE')>0, 'TRIPWIRE', countIf(verdict='REGRESSION')>0, 'REGRESSION', countIf(verdict='NO_DATA')=count(), 'NO_DATA', 'OK') AS tier1_verdict FROM v_kc_pair_ratios WHERE tier='1' AND pair_seq=1
charts.description: TIER 1 verdict tile for the latest pair. Rolls the Tier-1 gate (drain_rows_per_sec, banded ±8.5%; parts_per_insert, a binary TRIPWIRE) into one worst-case verdict. HOW TO READ: OK = drain throughput held its band AND parts_per_insert was exactly 1.0 (one part per insert, the structural invariant). TRIPWIRE = parts_per_insert deviated from 1.0 — batches are spraying across partitions; this ALERTS EVEN DURING CALIBRATION (it is an invariant break, not band noise). REGRESSION = drain throughput fell out of band in the bad direction (alerts only after calibration). DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: OK/IMPROVEMENT good; TRIPWIRE and REGRESSION bad (TRIPWIRE is the more urgent); NO_DATA/FLAGGED neither.

charts.query: SELECT if(countIf(integrity_ok = 0)=0 AND countIf(duplicate_rows>0)=0, 'OK', 'FAIL') AS integrity FROM v_kc_runs WHERE pair_id = (SELECT argMax(pair_id, run_started_at) FROM v_kc_runs)
charts.description: INTEGRITY tile for the latest pair. OK iff every run of the pair delivered exactly the expected rows (rows_delivered==rows_expected) with zero duplicates (duplicate_rows=0). HOW TO READ: OK = the ingest was lossless and dup-free, so the throughput numbers are trustworthy; FAIL = an integrity mismatch, which per contract §3 FAILS the run — it contributes NO headline number and the verdict tiles should be read as suspect. DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: OK is the only acceptable state; FAIL is a hard stop (contract precedence: FAIL overrides every band/tripwire verdict).

charts.query: SELECT if(countIf(flagged)=0, 'OK', toString(countIf(flagged))) AS validity_guards FROM v_kc_runs WHERE pair_id = (SELECT argMax(pair_id, run_started_at) FROM v_kc_runs)
charts.description: VALIDITY GUARDS tile for the latest pair (Kafka-specific twist, plan §8). OK iff no run of the pair tripped a validity guard (task retries, settle timeout, rebalance, task restart, drain incomplete, instrument_resize/shift). Otherwise shows the count of flagged runs. HOW TO READ: a guard trip is NOT a regression — it means the pair is non-comparable and is excluded from bands/ratios; the reason is in the flagged-run log below. DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: OK is good; any non-zero means investigate the log, but do NOT read it as a performance regression.

charts.query: SELECT concat(toString(uniqExactIf(pair_id, verdict IN ('OK','IMPROVEMENT'))), '/', toString(least(uniqExact(pair_id), 20))) AS pairs_in_band FROM v_kc_pair_ratios WHERE NOT flagged
charts.description: PAIRS IN BAND (n/20) tile — the calibration progress + health counter. Numerator = distinct unflagged pairs whose verdicts are OK or IMPROVEMENT (held their calibrated bands / tripwire); denominator = min(pairs so far, 20), the calibration target. HOW TO READ: while < 20 the whole dashboard is 'calibrating' and band-excursion alerts stay unwired (only integrity failures and the parts TRIPWIRE alert). At ~12 pairs the bands recalibrate from trailing-window statistics (contract §3 recalibration rule). DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: numerator approaching the denominator is good; the denominator climbing toward 20 marks progress out of the calibration hold.

charts.query: SELECT tier, metric_name, if(is_tripwire=1,'TRIPWIRE (head==1.0)', concat('±', toString(round(band*100,1)), '%')) AS rule, head_value, pinned_value, ratio, round((ratio-1)*100,2) AS delta_pct, verdict, alert_now FROM v_kc_pair_ratios WHERE pair_seq=1 ORDER BY tier, metric_name
charts.description: LATEST-PAIR gated-metrics table — the detail behind the two verdict tiles. One row per gated (tier, metric): the rule (calibrated band width, or 'TRIPWIRE' for parts_per_insert), HEAD value, pinned value, their ratio, the delta % ((ratio-1)*100), the verdict, and whether it alerts now. HOW TO READ: banded rows — delta_pct inside the rule's ± band ⇒ OK; outside in the good direction ⇒ IMPROVEMENT; outside in the bad direction ⇒ REGRESSION; blank ratio ⇒ NO_DATA (a metric missing or pinned=0). The parts_per_insert row is a TRIPWIRE: ignore its ratio — only the HEAD value matters (exactly 1.0 ⇒ OK; anything else ⇒ TRIPWIRE, alerts even while calibrating). merge_amplification is watch-only (Tab 2) and ch_avg_rows_per_insert is a covariate — neither appears here by design. DIRECTION OF GOODNESS per metric: null_drain/drain rows-per-sec are higher_better (want ratio ≥ 1); connect_cpu_seconds_per_Mrows is lower_better (want ratio ≤ 1). The verdict column already encodes direction — read verdict for the answer, ratio for the magnitude.

charts.filters: tier='0' AND NOT flagged AND ratio IS NOT NULL
charts.description: TIER 0 H/P RATIO TREND (one line per Tier-0 gated metric). Plots the head/pinned ratio per pair over time. The bands are PER METRIC (calibrated at 2x each metric's measured noise floor, contract Amendment 2026-07-09b): null_drain_rows_per_sec ±8.5% (outer reference lines at 0.915 and 1.085) and connect_cpu_seconds_per_Mrows ±6% (inner reference lines at 0.94 and 1.06). Environment noise hits both arms and cancels in the ratio, so a line leaving ITS band is a connector signal, not weather. HOW TO READ: match each line to its own band. A 'PROVISIONAL — calibrating (n=X/20)' watermark means excursions do NOT alert yet. Flagged pairs are omitted (see the log). DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: null_drain_rows_per_sec is higher_better (above its band = improvement, below = regression); connect_cpu_seconds_per_Mrows is lower_better (below its band = improvement, above = regression).

charts.filters: tier='1' AND NOT flagged AND ratio IS NOT NULL AND is_tripwire = 0
charts.description: TIER 1 H/P RATIO TREND. Plots the head/pinned ratio of drain_rows_per_sec (verified throughput) per pair over time, with its ±8.5% calibrated band shaded around 1.0 (contract Amendment 2026-07-09b). parts_per_insert is deliberately NOT on this chart — it is a binary TRIPWIRE on the head arm's absolute value (see the gated table / Tier-1 tile), not a ratio series; charting its ratio would invite reading band semantics into an invariant. HOW TO READ: inside the shaded band = OK; drifting out = a connector change moved verified throughput relative to the pinned baseline. 'PROVISIONAL — calibrating (n=X/20)' watermark = excursions do not alert yet. DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: drain_rows_per_sec is higher_better — above the band is an improvement, below the band is a regression.

charts.query: SELECT run_started_at, pair_id, tier, arm, log_class, flag_reason, outcome, batch_size FROM v_kc_flagged_log ORDER BY run_started_at DESC
charts.description: EXCURSION + FLAGGED-RUN LOG — the audit trail of every run kept OUT of the trends and why. One row per flagged (validity guard tripped) or failed (integrity/outcome) run of connector kafka-connect. HOW TO READ: log_class=FLAGGED with flag_reason='instrument_resize' (e.g. pair 2026-07-08T11-52-46Z-e140231) means the measurement harness's compute changed, so that pair is excluded from bands/ratios and band calibration restarts on the new instrument — this is expected, not a regression. log_class=FAILED means an integrity mismatch or a failed ingest (no headline number). DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: an EMPTY log is the healthy state; entries here explain gaps in the ratio trends and should be triaged by reason, never read as performance regressions.
